data.title: NameSilo enters LOI to acquire Reach Systems for C$4.5M
Apple Intelligence push into China gets closer with new feedback form: report
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) bringing its Apple Intelligence features to China may be getting closer after a new feedback form recently popped up on the tech giant's website, MacRumors reported.

The feedback form asked users for an +86 phone number, with +86 being the country code for China, the news outlet added, citing the form. The form has since been removed from Apple's website, but Seeking Alpha was able to independently verify the form's existence and the need for an +86 phone number prior to removal.

Apple did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Apple introduced Apple Intelligence in 2024 at its annual developers conference. Since then, the company has come under some criticism for a lack of features with Apple Intelligence, which requires an iPhone 16 (now obsolete) or later.

Alibaba (BABA) Chairman Joseph Tsai said in February 2025 that iPhones would use Alibaba's AI technology to aid with Apple Intelligence in China. However, the launch of the features was reportedly pushed back by the trade war between the U.S. and China.

In May, Bloomberg reported that Apple could launch Apple Intelligence in July as part of its iOS 18.6 update, but that did not happen. The next iOS update Apple is scheduled to launch is iOS 26.2, which is set to happen before the end of the year.

Apple is set to partner with Alibaba and Baidu (BIDU) for Apple Intelligence, with Alibaba's models being used for the censorship engine and Baidu's models used for Siri and Visual Intelligence. In the U.S., Apple has partnered with OpenAI for Siri integration.

An upgraded version of Siri is likely to be a part of iOS 26.4, which is set for some time in early 2026.

data.title: Elekta AB Non-GAAP EPS of SEK 0.64, revenue of SEK 4.07B; reaffirms FY outlook
data.description:
• Elekta AB press release (EKTAF): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of SEK 0.64.
  • Revenue of SEK 4.07B (-6.2% Y/Y).
  • The book-to-bill ratio was 1.0 (0.99), rolling twelve months ended at 1.09 (1.09).
  • Higher adjusted gross margin of 37.9 percent
  • Cash flow after continuous investments amounted to SEK 358 M (-31), an improvement of SEK 389 M YoY.
  • Implementation of a new operating model has been initiated aiming for increased velocity of product development, commercial execution and operational excellence to better serve customers and patients.
  • As a consequence of the new operating model, a simplified and decentralized organization will be implemented leading to yearly cost savings of no less than SEK 500 M with the full effect starting Q1 2026/27.
  • Outlook"We reiterate our full-year 2025/26 outlook, where we expect net sales in constant currency to grow year-over-year. Sales in China are expected to start to recover during the second half of 2025/26. Furthermore, we expect continuous negative impact on earnings from FX at current exchange rates and tariffs."

data.title: YieldMax to execute reverse stock splits of 15 option income strategy funds
data.description:
YieldMax ETFs is set to execute reverse stock splits for 15 of its option income strategy funds, effective prior to market open on Dec. 1, Dec. 2, and Dec. 8, 2025.

The reverse stock splits will increase the price per share of each fund with a proportionate decrease in the number of shares outstanding.

Reverse splits taking effect December 1, 2025:

  • YieldMax MRNA Option Income Strategy ETF (MRNY) reverse split ratio of 1:10
  • YieldMax Ultra Option Income Strategy ETF (ULTY) reverse split ratio of 1:10
  • YieldMax TSLA Option Income Strategy ETF (TSLY) reverse split ratio of 1:5
  • YieldMax XYZ Option Income Strategy ETF (XYZY) reverse split ratio of 1:5
  • YieldMax Bitcoin Option Income Strategy ETF (YBIT) reverse split ratio of 1:5

Reverse splits taking effect December 2, 2025:

  • YieldMax Short COIN Option Income Strategy ETF (FIAT) reverse split ratio of 1:10
  • YieldMax AI Option Income Strategy ETF (AIYY) reverse split ratio of 1:10
  • YieldMax Short TSLA Option Income Strategy ETF (CRSH) reverse split ratio of 1:10
  • YieldMax Short NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF (DIPS) reverse split ratio of 1:10
  • YieldMax COIN Option Income Strategy ETF (CONY) reverse split ratio of 1:10
  • YieldMax Innovation Option Income Strategy ETF (OARK) reverse split ratio of 1:5
  • YieldMax ABNB Option Income Strategy ETF (ABNY) reverse split ratio of 1:5

Reverse splits taking effect December 8, 2025:

  • YieldMax AMD Option Income Strategy ETF (AMDY) reverse split ratio of 1:5
  • YieldMax MSTR Option Income Strategy ETF (MSTY) reverse split ratio of 1:5
  • YieldMax PYPL Option Income Strategy ETF (PYPY) reverse split ratio of 1:5

data.title: Currency Exchange announces renewal of share buyback program and automatic securities purchase plan
data.description:
Currency Exchange International (OTCPK:CURN) on Wednesday announced acceptance by the Toronto Stock Exchange of Currency Exchange’s notice of intention to renew its normal course issuer bid and Automatic Securities Purchase Plan to purchase for cancellation a maximum amount of 359,617 common shares.

Purchases under the NCIB may commence on December 2, 2025, and will terminate on December 1, 2026, or at such earlier date if the maximum number of shares sought in the NCIB has been repurchased.

data.title: Primark could be set free from Associated British Foods in a spinoff
data.description:
Associated British Foods (OTCPK:ASBFY) is exploring a structural separation in which Primark would be split from the group’s food operations and potentially be floated as a standalone company that would be likely to be traded in London. 

The strategic review by Associated British Foods (OTCPK:ASBFY) is being driven by Primark’s growing scale and profitability relative to the company's slower-growth and more cyclical sugar and broader food businesses, along with a desire to give investors clearer visibility on each side of the group. Under the scenario being discussed, Associated British Foods (OTCPK:ASBFY) and controlling shareholder Wittington Investments would aim to retain majority ownership in both Primark and the remaining food-focused company, making the move a demerger or spin-off intended to unlock value rather than a full sale. 

A Primark listing is seen highlighting the retailer's ~£10 billion revenue base and expanding international footprint.

Primark is an international value-focused fashion retailer that is known for selling on-trend clothing, accessories, beauty products, and homeware at attractive price points to consumers. The brand was founded in Ireland in 1969 under the Penneys name and has grown into one of Europe’s largest clothing retailers, with hundreds of stores across the UK, Ireland, and continental Europe.

 Primark has also been building out its U.S. footprint over the past decade, using a pure-brick-and-mortar, value-fashion proposition aimed at budget-conscious families. Currently, Primark operates roughly 33 stores across more than 12 U.S. states. Notably, Primark also recently began to operate in the Middle East under a franchise model.

data.title: Google’s AI chips 'great thing' but will not shake Nvidia: Wedbush
data.description:
Wedbush said Google’s Tensor Processing Units, or TPUs, are not shaking Nvidia's (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang, following reports that Meta Platforms (META) is in talks to spend billions on the Alphabet (GOOGL) (GOOG) unit's AI chips.

"Google's TPU chips along with its key partner Broadcom (AVGO) are now front and center and gaining some clear momentum in the AI market and this is a great thing...just like AMD's (AMD) recent success and we will see in the future with other Big Tech stalwarts getting in the AI chip Race and monetization around this 4th Industrial Revolution. That said, the AI Revolution starts and end with Nvidia today and that is not changing for another few years," said analysts led by Daniel Ives.

The analysts noted that with the trillions set to be spent over the coming years, many big tech players will benefit besides Nvidia on the chip front. However, "that should not be mistaken for "Nvidia being the indisputable Rocky Balboa champion of the AI Revolution and that is not changing anytime soon on the chip front."

The analysts added that it's been a building crescendo moment for tech stocks that hit another turbulent moment when Nvidia last week put up strong growth numbers and blew away Street estimates, and the stock sold off the next day and continues to tread water with the latest GoogleTPU news adding to some pressure on the stock.

"This added to fears around the "AI Bubble" talk/chatter from the bears along with worries about Nvidia China revenues being restricted (although recent signs of progress from the Beltway) andfears of the "too big to fail" OpenAI chatter and circular financing...with the Burry short soap opera adding to the agita," said Ives and his team.

However, the analysts noted that they view this as a short lived digestion moment for tech stocks as they believe tech stocks will have a rally into the rest of the year as investors look to play the "AI Revolution and the 2nd/3rd/4th derivatives now just starting to play out across consumer and enterprise names."

"This is an AI Arms Race and what is fueling this next chapter of growth is Big Tech spending and that is NOT slowing down into 2026 with Nvidia's bullish quarter/guidance/ commentary our major focal point as a huge piece in the AI puzzle into 2026," said Ives and his team.

data.title: Li Auto swings to a loss in Q3 due to supply chain bottlenecks, lower selling prices, and a biting recall
data.description:
Li Auto (LI) fell in early trading on Wednesday after issuing its third-quarter earnings report.

Total deliveries for the quarter were down 39.0% to 93,211 vehicles, which contributed to the Chinese electric vehicle maker swinging to a loss of RMB625 million during the quarter.

Vehicle margin fell to 15.5% of sales in the quarter, compared with 20.9% in the third quarter of 2024 and 19.4% in the second quarter of 2025. The loss from operations during the quarter was RMB1.2 billion ($165.4 million), compared with RMB3.4 billion in income from operations a year ago. Free cash was also negative in Q3.

"While navigating intensifying market competition, we faced headwinds in the third quarter from supply chain bottlenecks and costs related to the recall of Li MEGA," highlighted CFO Tie Li. "We have been proactively working with our supply chain partners to fulfill the demand of our users for Li i8 and Li i6," he added. Looking ahead, Li said the company will maintain its commitment to product and technology innovation, delivering exceptional user experiences while driving sustainable growth.

At the end of the quarter, Li Auto (LI) had 542 retail stores in 157 cities, 546 servicing centers and Li Auto-authorized body and paint shops operating in 225 cities, and 3,420 supercharging stations in operation equipped with 18,897 charging stalls.

Morgan Stanley analyst Tim Hsiao characterized the earnings release as a modest shortfall in comparison to consensus, but the firm did not change its bullish thesis on Li Auto (LI).

Shares of Li Auto (LI) were down 2.3% in premarket trading on Wednesday.
